Bash(cmd.exe /c "gh pr create --title ""feat: dynamic PDXScript map parsing for svealand_area"" --body ""## Summary\\n\\n- Adds **Tamar.Clausewitz 0.5.1** as a NuGet dependency to extract-map.csx, replacing the inline parser — requires the script to run under .NET 10 \(dotnet-script reinstalled accordingly\)\\n- Parses definitions.txt using the Clausewitz DOM API \(.Clauses for nested blocks, .Tokens for bare location identifiers\) to extract the full svealand_area province → location hierarchy\\n- Parses all named_locations/*.txt files using .Bindings to build a dynamic location → RGB hex colour lookup, replacing the previous hardcoded colour array\\n- Switches the image pixel scan from a linear colour search to an O\(1\) Dictionary lookup\\n- Replaces the SVG output with a structured svealand_area.json containing the full hierarchy: area → provinces → locations → { name, color, paths[] }\\n\\n## Test plan\\n\\n- [ ] Run dotnet-script Scripts/extract-map.csx from the EU5MapExplorer.Api directory\\n- [ ] Verify Step 1 console output lists 7 provinces with correct location counts\\n- [ ] Verify Step 2 loads colour entries without errors\\n- [ ] Verify Scripts/svealand_area.json is produced with all provinces, locations, colours and traced paths populated\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code]\(https://claude.com/claude-code\)"" 2>&1")
